prompt: Build a FulfillOps Rewards & Compliance Console that manages reward-tier fulfillment end-to-end for an offline operations team running onsite campaigns and member perks. Users include Administrators who configure reward tiers and system policies, Fulfillment Specialists who pack and ship physical items or issue e-vouchers, and Auditors who review immutable activity history and exports. The Templ-rendered web UI presents a dashboard with today's pending fulfillments, overdue exceptions, and threshold alerts, plus a searchable message center and reporting workspace; every action provides immediate in-app feedback (success, validation error, or "queued" for scheduled jobs) and a visible status timeline. Reward tiers support inventory counts, per-customer purchase limits (default 2 per tier per 30 days), and shipping information maintenance using US address formats; a fulfillment record transitions through Draft, Ready to Ship, Shipped, Delivered, Voucher Issued, Completed, On Hold, and Canceled, with a required reason for holds/cancellations. Physical shipments store carrier name and tracking number (validated as alphanumeric 8–30 chars) and e-vouchers store voucher code plus optional expiration. Overdue alerts trigger when Ready to Ship items are not shipped within 48 hours or when vouchers are not issued within 4 business hours; exceptions open a work-order-style thread where staff document resolution steps. The message center uses locally stored templates for booking results, changes, expirations, and fulfillment progress, supports configurable delivery channels (in-app always on; SMS/email appear as "queued/printed" outputs for offline handoff), retries failed sends up to 3 times over 30 minutes, and lets staff query send logs by date range and recipient. The backend is implemented in Go using Gin to expose REST-style endpoints consumed by the Templ UI, with PostgreSQL as the system of record for reward tiers, fulfillment orders, message templates, send logs, reports, and audit trails. Data consistency is enforced with explicit transaction boundaries: updates that link plan-to-task style entities (tier inventory decrement, fulfillment creation, status transition, and notification enqueue) must commit atomically or roll back together, and optimistic locking (row version integers) prevents concurrent overwrite when two specialists edit the same fulfillment. Scheduled jobs run locally and are monitorable from an admin health screen, including nightly stats refresh at 2:00 AM, overdue reminder dispatch every 15 minutes, and report generation on configured cadences; each job writes run history and error stacks for exception recovery. Audit compliance includes operation logs for key tables capturing who changed what and when, soft delete with a 30-day recovery window, and masked display of sensitive fields (e.g., show only last 4 of phone numbers and partial addresses) while storing full values encrypted at rest using AES-256 with a locally managed key. Exporting reports requires explicit permission, writes an export audit record (who, when, filters used), and produces files to a local directory with checksum fingerprints. Operations support configuration-driven environments, on-demand health checks, scheduled backups for database and attached media assets to an offline path, quarterly disaster recovery drills, and one-click restore that rehydrates PostgreSQL and verifies referential integrity before reopening the system.
